//: Single source of truth for the generated Rust API reference. Consumed by BOTH the CI doc
//2: generator (scripts/build-api-docs.sh, via jq) and the /docs/api bridge page (api.astro).
//3: `pkg` is the crate name; its rustdoc directory is pkg with '-' replaced by '_'. `features`
//4: (optional) is passed to `cargo doc --features` for crates that need one backend to compile
//5: (only the `day` umbrella does — the headless `mock` backend keeps the doc build portable).
//6: `repo`/`branch` point rustdoc's Source links at GitHub (see scripts/rustdoc-github-source.py).

groups.name: Core
groups.blurb: The framework your app is built on.

crates.blurb: The umbrella crate your app depends on — re-exports the core API plus one native backend.
The Piece model: the realized view tree, the mounter, the layout engine, and event routing.
The Copy-signal reactive core — signals, memos, and effects that keep a built-once tree live.
The built-in piece library (text, stacks, controls, list…) and the renderer! extension macro.
Plain Copy value types — points, sizes, rects, and affine transforms — shared across backends.
The backend-neutral toolkit specification that pieces emit and each toolkit renders.
The piece-facing Project Fluent localization layer over the day-l10n engine.
The low-level localization engine and the built-in core string catalog.
The embedded dayscript engine that drives scripted UI walkthroughs and tests.
The headless toolkit — a backend with no native UI, for tests and documentation.

groups.name: Pieces
groups.blurb: Optional native widgets you add to an app à la carte.

crates.blurb: An editable combo box: free text with a native dropdown of suggestions.
A native search input with a search glyph and a clear button.
A tappable star-rating control, composed from primitive pieces.
An embedded native web view — WKWebView, WebView2, WebKitGTK, or Android WebView.
Each platform's native media/video player, behind one Rust API.
A native image view that decodes and caches an image from a URL.
The platform's native map view (Apple platforms).
A Lottie vector animation, on iOS and Android.
The platform's native share / activity sheet.

groups.name: Parts
groups.blurb: Headless native capabilities — no UI; any Rust code can depend on them.

crates.blurb: Battery level and charging state.
Motion sensors — accelerometer, gyroscope, and friends.
Read and write the system plain-text clipboard.
Network connectivity and connection type.
Trigger native haptic feedback.
A persistent native key/value preference store.
Device identity — model, OS version, and locale.
